summary: McKenzie notices an unexpected spike in downloads for Football Bingo cards and can't identify a referral source, concluding it must be organic search traffic. He discovered the anomaly while researching long tail search patterns for an upcoming blog post. The brief post illustrates how niche software can attract surprising audiences through search alone.
key_points: Unexpected organic search traffic can drive significant downloads for niche content without any identifiable referral links
Long tail search patterns produce anomalies that reveal untapped demand in surprising niches
Monitoring analytics data closely reveals opportunities that would otherwise go unnoticed
money_quotes: Several hundred people have started downloading Football Bingo all of a sudden. Not that I mind, of course, but I can't square it with what I know about my target users.
It seems to just be organic search traffic, and a whole mess of it.

I only noticed this because I'm writing one of my longer, 'evergreen content' pieces on the nature of the Long Tail (and how to extract money from it) and I've been chasing down some of the anomalies in my data.
key_insight: Organic search traffic can surface unexpected demand in niches you never anticipated, which is exactly the long tail phenomenon at work.
